Return trip from Fremont across town back to home; allows buffer for late afternoon traffic.

diary:
Started the day slow with coffee and toast in Redmond, then drove out to Ballard Farmers Market on a whim. The overcast sky was perfect for wandering—not too hot. Spent a couple hours picking through vendor stalls, grabbed some beautiful strawberries and heirloom tomatoes. One vendor was telling me about her farm up north; felt nice to actually talk to someone about where food comes from instead of just grabbing it off a shelf.

Hopped over to T-Mobile Park for the 1pm Mariners game. The crowd was solid but not overwhelming. Had a mediocre hot dog, but the game was entertaining enough. By the time I left, I was itching to move around again.

Ended up in Fremont on the way home—caught the tail end of the market there. Got some street tacos and just sat on a bench watching people mill around. The neighborhood has this laid-back energy I needed. Drove back to Redmond and cooked dinner with the day's haul. Felt grounded.
thought_process: I think I was craving something unstructured today. Work has been pretty intense lately, so I deliberately chose wandering over planning—farmers markets felt right for that. The Mariners game was more about being outside and around people than actually caring who won. I noticed I kept gravitating toward the markets and food vendors because there's something tangible about it, something real compared to sitting at a desk debugging code all week. The overcast weather probably helped too; it made everything feel less rushed. By Fremont, I was genuinely relaxed. Cooking with fresh ingredients felt like the natural conclusion—taking the day's discoveries and making something my own.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
